summary: Rewrote zo-101-briefing.md in V-voice (third-person, investor-focused, conversational). Structured: Who V Is → What Zo Is → Why It Matters → What V Built → FAQ (10 questions) → Next Steps. Injected 4 voice primitives (isn't X/it's Y, gap between great and good enough, 10x richer, hotel vs house analogy). Created voice-primitives-reference.md documenting injection points and usage guidance. Word count ~1,500 (well under 3K limit). No marketing speak, no hedge words, sensitive questions redirect to booking a call.
learnings: The 'isn't X... it's Y' syntactic pattern is the strongest spoken-cadence primitive for Frank — it creates natural contrast without sounding scripted
FAQ answers for sensitive data (user count, funding, roadmap) should always redirect to booking a call — builds trust and creates conversion funnel
Voice primitives should be capped at 3-4 per briefing version; more than that sounds over-styled
Briefing reads best when each section has one core idea and no section exceeds 4 short paragraphs
